name: Kip Filet with Apples and Red Currants
recipeInstructions:
1. A "kip filet" is a skinless, boneless chicken breast half.
2. Remove the roots and ugly leaves from the leeks.
3. Slice into thin rings.
4. Rinse in a colander.
5. Let drain.
6. Rinse the currants.
7. Use a fork to remove the currants from their stems.
8. In a deep bowl, mix together the egg, mustard, and flour.
9. Season the kip filets with celery salt and pepper.
10. Melt the butter in a frying pan over medium heat.
11. Coat the chicken evenly with the egg mixture.
12. Cook, turning occasionally, until golden brown on both sides, about 10 minutes total.
13. Meanwhile, peel and core the apple, then cut into thin slices.
14. Remove the chicken from the pan and keep warm in a serving dish.
15. Add the leeks to the pan and cook, stirring, for about 2 minutes.
16. Add the currants and apple slices; continue cooking another minute until warmed through.
17. Transfer to a serving plate.
18. Arrange the chicken filets on top.
19. Sprinkle with chopped parsley.
